title: parent-flag-bad-hex-silent: --parent <bad-7-hex> silently matches nothing instead of issue_not_found
body:
# Goal

`jjf ls --parent <handle>` / `jjf ready --parent <handle>` /
`jjf search --parent <handle>` should surface `issue_not_found`
(exit 1) when `<handle>` is a well-formed 7-char hex id that
doesn't match any issue. Today they silently match nothing
(empty result, exit 0), which is a footgun for operators who
typo a parent id.

# Repro

```
$ jjf ls --parent abcdef0
# returns nothing, exit 0 â€” but no such issue exists
```

(Slug handles already exit 2 with `slug_not_found` correctly;
this bug is specific to the well-formed-hex-but-nonexistent path.)

# Source

Final whole-branch review of the `--parent` feature (2026-06-28
drop-epic-label) flagged this as Minor #2. The root cause is
that `resolve_handle` (`crates/jjf/src/main.rs:~2227` calling
`storage.resolve` at `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs:~3336`)
short-circuits on `IssueId::parse(handle).is_ok()` without an
existence check. The other id-consuming verbs (`jjf show`,
`jjf comment`, `jjf update`, ...) do a downstream `storage.read`
that surfaces `issue_not_found`, but the filter callers don't
read â€” they just pass the id into a predicate, so the bad id
silently matches no issues.

This is a small deviation from the spec's "Unknown handle exits
2 (`slug_not_found`)" â€” the spec was silent on the hex-but-
nonexistent case.

# Fix options

- **A.** In `run_ls` / `run_ready` / `run_search`, after the
  `resolve_handle` call, do a `storage.read(&parent_id)?` to
  force an existence check. Bad id surfaces as `issue_not_found`
  exit 1, same as `jjf show <bad-id>`.
- **B.** Document the behavior in `docs/cli-json.md` under each
  `--parent <H>` bullet ("unknown id silently matches nothing;
  only an unknown slug exits 2"). Cheaper, leaves footgun.

Recommend A. The cost is one extra `Storage::read` per --parent
invocation, which is negligible (one ref lookup).

# Acceptance

- [ ] `jjf ls --parent <bad-7-hex>` exits 1 with
      `issue_not_found`.
- [ ] Same for `jjf ready --parent <bad-7-hex>` and
      `jjf search --parent <bad-7-hex>`.
- [ ] `jjf ls --parent <unknown-slug>` still exits 2 with
      `slug_not_found` (unchanged).
- [ ] `jjf ls --parent <good-handle>` still works (unchanged).
- [ ] New CLI tests pinning the exit-1 path for each verb.
